title: AGENTS.md
text: feature, fix, refactor: Update packages: pak::pak() Run existing tests — confirm everything passes touching code: devtools::test(reporter = "check") tests fail, stop ask user proceed. Plan — identify R files affected; check whether new exported functions needed (→ r-code skill) Test first — write failing test, implement (→ tdd-workflow skill): devtools::test(filter = "name", reporter = "check") — fail Implement — minimal code make tests pass Refactor — clean keeping tests green Document — new changed exported functions, use document skill Verify: run air format . , just wrapping : devtools::check(error_on = "warning"). Warnings errors must resolved. NOTEs resolved . News — add bullet top NEWS.md (development version heading). Rules: user-facing changes; skip purely internal changes. One line per bullet; end full stop. Write users, developers. Frame positively, present tense: * `FetchRepoIssues()` now accepts ... * Fixed bug ... Put function name (backticks ()) near start. Issue number contributor go parentheses final period: * `FetchRepoIssues()` now accepts ... (@{username}, #{issue_number}). Get username: gh api user --jq .login

title: User acceptance testing with ExpectUserAccepts
text: Suppose issue #42 documents aesthetic problem: HTML report header uses outdated brand colors. developer creates branch fix-42, implements CSS changes, needs sign-non-technical reviewer fix considered complete. developer 🧑‍💻 adds test: strDescription: short summary becomes sub-issue title. intIssue: parent GitHub issue number (#42). Even enclosing test_that() links multiple issues, must single issue number. chrInstructions: optional context reviewer, link preview deployment. chrChecks: checkbox items reviewer see sub-issue body. chrAssignees: GitHub username(s) reviewer(s) assign. parameter defaults qcthat_UAT_ASSIGNEES environment variable, allowing set assignees dynamically automated checks. recommend leaving assignee blank individual test setting dynamically GitHub action, described Environment variables.
code: test_that("Report header uses the updated brand colors (#42)", {   ExpectUserAccepts(     strDescription = "Report header uses updated brand colors",     intIssue = 42L,     chrInstructions = "Open the report at https://example.com/preview and inspect the header.",     chrChecks = c(       "Header background is #59488f",       "Logo is centered and not clipped",       "Font renders as Montserrat on the title"     ),     chrAssignees = "design-reviewer"   ) })

title: User acceptance testing with ExpectUserAccepts
text: test runs, ExpectUserAccepts() performs following steps: Guard checks. function executes CRAN, inside git repository, online. Otherwise silently returns without side effects. Issue lookup. Searches existing child issue #42 qcthat-uat label matching title ("qcthat Acceptance #42: Report header uses updated brand colors"). Issue creation. matching child issue exists, creates one sub-issue #42 : title body containing: “Close issue indicate acceptance.”, chrInstructions text, checkbox items chrChecks qcthat-uat label Assignment. Assigns specified GitHub user(s). issue previously closed new assignee added, issue automatically re-opened. State check. sub-issue closed: testthat::pass(). sub-issue open: testthat::fail(), lglReportFailure TRUE. default controlled qcthat_UAT environment variable (see Environment variables). lglReportFailure FALSE, expectation skipped (returns NULL without signalling condition, expectations test_that() block still run). Logging. Records result internal registry used CommentUAT() post status reports pull requests. local development, test fail default qcthat_UAT environment variable set. reports failures GitHub Actions workflow qcthat_UAT: true configured.

title: User acceptance testing with ExpectUserAccepts
text: qcthat.yaml workflow (installed via use_qcthat()) handles automated side: qcthat-uat labeled issue closed, workflow fires issues: [closed] event. workflow runs TriggerUAT(), finds open pull requests referencing closed issue. workflow run already progress PRs, QC workflow re-triggered. re-run, test suite executes . time ExpectUserAccepts() sees sub-issue closed calls testthat::pass(). UAT report comment PR updated reflect accepted state. means reviewer need understand R, testthat, CI system. just close GitHub issue.

title: User acceptance testing with ExpectUserAccepts
text: Keep strDescription short specific — becomes sub-issue title. Use chrInstructions link reviewer preview deployment specific page report. Multiple ExpectUserAccepts() calls can exist test file (even withing test_that() block), tracking different aspect issue different issues. can set assignees via qcthat_UAT_ASSIGNEES environment variable workflow YAML instead hard-coding usernames. allows different assignees per branch target:
code: # In your workflow YAML: # qcthat_UAT_ASSIGNEES: "design-reviewer,product-owner"  # In your test — uses the env var by default: test_that("dashboard layout matches mockup (#55)", {   ExpectUserAccepts(     strDescription = "Dashboard layout matches mockup",     intIssue = 55L,     chrChecks = c(       "Sidebar collapses on mobile",       "Charts are responsive"     )   ) })

title: Does a user accept the feature? — ExpectUserAccepts
code: ExpectUserAccepts(   strDescription,   intIssue,   chrInstructions = character(),   chrChecks = character(),   lglReportFailure = IsCheckingUAT(),   chrAssignees = Sys.getenv("qcthat_UAT_ASSIGNEES"),   strOwner = GetGHOwner(),   strRepo = GetGHRepo(),   strGHToken = gh::gh_token() )

title: Does a user accept the feature? — ExpectUserAccepts
text: strDescription (length-1 character) brief description user expectation. intIssue (length-1 integer) issue check associated. chrInstructions (character) Instructions review issue. Included associated issue checklist. chrChecks (character) Items user check. preceded checkboxes associated issue. lglReportFailure (length-1 logical) Whether ignore failures (default unless "qcthat_UAT" environment variable "true"), fail (show failure testthat tests). chrAssignees (character) GitHub usernames issue associated expectation assigned. Whenever issue assigned new user, re-opened. Elements vector split commas, can provide multiple assignees single string. helpful like set assignees via "qcthat_UAT_ASSIGNEES" environment variable, checked default. strOwner (length-1 character) GitHub username organization name. strRepo (length-1 character) GitHub repository name. strGHToken (length-1 character) GitHub token permissions appropriate action performed.

title: Does a user accept the feature? — ExpectUserAccepts
text: input strDescription, invisibly.

title: Does a user accept the feature? — ExpectUserAccepts
text: called outside CRAN, inside git repository, internet connection, ExpectUserAccepts() performs following steps: Looks existing child issue intIssue labeled "qcthat-uat" whose title matches strDescription. issue exists, creates one sub-issue title "qcthat Acceptance #N: {strDescription}", body containing chrInstructions checkbox items chrChecks, "qcthat-uat" label. Assigns issue chrAssignees (re-opening closed new assignee added). Checks issue state: Closed: calls testthat::pass(). Open: calls testthat::fail() lglReportFailure TRUE (controlled qcthat_UAT environment variable via IsCheckingUAT()). Logs result use UAT reports (see CommentUAT()). guard condition met (CRAN, git repo, offline), function silently returns strDescription without side effects.

title: Does a user accept the feature? — ExpectUserAccepts
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ test_that("report uses updated brand colors (#42)", {   ExpectUserAccepts(     strDescription = "Report header uses updated brand colors",     intIssue = 42L,     chrChecks = c(       "Header background is #59488f",       "Logo is centered and not clipped"     ),     chrAssignees = "design-reviewer"   ) }) } # }

title: Parameters used in multiple functions — shared-params
text: chrAssignees (character) GitHub usernames issue associated expectation assigned. Whenever issue assigned new user, re-opened. Elements vector split commas, can provide multiple assignees single string. helpful like set assignees via "qcthat_UAT_ASSIGNEES" environment variable, checked default. chrChecks (character) Items user check. preceded checkboxes associated issue. chrClass (character) Class name(s) assign object. chrCommitSHAs (character) SHAs git commits. chrErrorMessage (character) message include error report. Can include glue::glue() syntax. Formatted via cli::cli_bullets(). chrIgnoredLabels (character) GitHub labels ignore, "qcthat-nocov". chrInstructions (character) Instructions review issue. Included associated issue checklist. chrKeywords (character) Keywords search just issue numbers commit messages. Defaults GitHub issue-linking keywords chrLabels (character) name(s) labels(s) use. chrMilestones (character) name(s) milestone(s) filter issues . chrSourcePath (character) Components path source file. file extension included filename omitted (sent separate piece vector). chrTargetPath (character) Components path target file. file extension included filename omitted (sent separate piece vector). chrTestLines (character) Vector lines test file. chrTests (character) vector test descriptions CompileIssueTestMatrix() matrix extracted test files. dttmTimestamp (POSIXct) system timestamp. dfFileTests (data.frame) tibble::tibble() structure returned ExtractTestsFromFiles(). dfIssueCommitsLong (data.frame NULL) Pre-computed issue-commit mappings MapLongIssueCommits(). NULL (default), fetched automatically GitHub API. Provide calling MapTestFilesToPotentialIssues() multiple times avoid redundant API requests. dfITM (qcthat_IssueTestMatrix) qcthat_IssueTestMatrix object returned AsIssueTestMatrix() (often via QCPackage()). dfLabels (data.frame) data frame columns Label, Description, Color, specifying labels create. default, data frame returned DefaultIgnoreLabelsDF(). Descriptions labels created via function prefixed "{qcthat}: " make easier search list labels. dfPotentialIssues (tibble) data frame returned MapTestFilesToPotentialIssues(). dfRepoIssues (qcthat_Issues data frame) Data frame GitHub issues returned FetchRepoIssues(). dfTestResults (qcthat_TestResults data frame) Data frame test results returned CompileTestResults(). envCall (environment) environment use error reporting. Typically set rlang::caller_env(). envErrorMessage (environment) environment pass glue::glue() resolve variables. envPkg (environment) loaded package environment, returned LoadPkgEnv(). fctDisposition (factor) Disposition factor levels "fail", "warn", "skip", "pass". intIssue (length-1 integer) issue check associated. intIssues (integer) vector issue numbers CompileIssueTestMatrix() matrix GitHub. intLineEnd (length-1 integer) ending line number. intLineStart (length-1 integer) starting line number. intMaxCommits (length-1 integer) maximum number commits return git logs. Leaving default almost always fine, can reduce number repository long commit history function slow. intPageMax (length-1 integer) maximum number pages commits fetch GitHub API. page contains 100 commits. Defaults 100, fetches 10,000 commits. likely never need increase number, try larger number merge involves large number commits large repository. intParentIssue (length-1 integer) number parent issue parent-child issue relationship. intPRNumber (length-1 integer) number pull request fetch information /post results . intPRNumbers (integer) vector pull request numbers. intTestStart (length-1 integer) Line number test_that starts. intUATIssue (integer) number issue tracks user-acceptance testing. lCommentsRaw (list) List raw comment objects returned gh::gh(). lGHEventPayload (list) GitHub event payload. Defaults result LoadGHEventPayload(). lIssuesNonPR (list) List issue objects returned RemovePRsFromIssues(). lTestResults (testthat_results) testthat test results object, typically obtained running something like testthat::test_local() stop_on_failure = FALSE reporter cause issues parallel testing, like reporter = "silent", assigning name. lPRActionRuns (list) list workflow run objects returned GitHub. lglCompleted (length-1 logical) Whether include QCCompletedIssues() report. lglMilestone (length-1 logical) Whether include QCMilestones() report. lglOverwrite (length-1 logical) Whether overwrite files already exist. lglPR (length-1 logical) Whether include QCPR() report. lglReportFailure (length-1 logical) Whether ignore failures (default unless "qcthat_UAT" environment variable "true"), fail (show failure testthat tests). lglShowMilestones (length-1 logical) Whether separate issues milestones reports. lglShowIgnoredLabels (length-1 logical) Whether show information reports issue labels ("qcthat-nocov") ignored. lglUAT (length-1 logical) Whether include CommentUAT() report. lglUpdate (length-1 logical) Whether update existing comment label already exists (rather creating new comment label). lglUseCoverage (length-1 logical) Whether augment potential issues source-line coverage. TRUE, covr::environment_coverage() used discover issues commits touched source code exercised test. Requires covr installed. Defaults FALSE. lglUseEmoji (length-1 logical) Whether use emojis (TRUE emoji package installed) ASCII indicators (FALSE) output. default, determined qcthat.emoji option, defaults TRUE. lglWarn (length-1 logical) Whether warn extra value included filter (report still returns results). Defaults TRUE. objShape (0-row data.frame, etc) Object expected structure. strBody (length-1 character) body issue, PR, comment, release, GitHub markdown. strBodyCompiled (length-1 character) full body issue, PR, comment, GitHub markdown, including components title hidden qcthat-comment-id. strChildIssueID (length-1 character) id field issue connect parent issue. strCommentID (length-1 character) unique ID comment within given context, usually hash title comment. strCommitSHA (length-1 character) commit SHA target. strConditionSubclass (length-1 character) subclass condition. strConditionClass (length-1 character) One "error", "warning", "message". strDescription (length-1 character) brief description user expectation. strDirPath (length-1 character) path directory. strDisposition (length-1 character) result test, "pass", "fail", "warn", "skip", "accepted" "pending" UAT. strErrorSubclass (length-1 character) subclass error condition. strExtension (length-1 character) file extension use target file. target path already includes extension, replaced value. value already correct, effect. strFile (length-1 character) file name without path. strFilePath (length-1 character) file path. strGHToken (length-1 character) GitHub token permissions appropriate action performed. strLabel (length-1 character) name label create update. strLabelColor (length-1 character) hex color code label (e.g., "#444444"). strLabelDescription (length-1 character) description label. strLabelNewName (length-1 character) new name updated label. strOwner (length-1 character) GitHub username organization name. strPkgRoot (length-1 character) path directory package. expanded using gert::git_find(). strPRHeadRef (length-1 character) branch name (head ref) PR. strReleaseID (length-1 character) ID (typically numeric can long) GitHub release. strRepo (length-1 character) GitHub repository name. strReportType (length-1 character) main title report, "Completed Issues" "PR-Associated Issues". strRunID (length-1 character) ID (typically numeric can long) GitHub Actions workflow run. strSourceRef (length-1 character) Name git reference contains changes. Defaults active branch repository. strState (length-1 character) State issues pull requests fetch. Must one "open", "closed", "". Defaults "open" pull requests "" issues. strStateReason (length-1 character) reason state change ("closed" strState). Must one "completed", "not_planned", "duplicate", "reopened.", NULL. strTagName (length-1 character) Name GitHub tag. strTargetDir (length-1 character) Path directory file added. strTargetRef (length-1 character) Name git reference merged . Defaults default branch repository. strTestDir (length-1 character) Path directory containing test files. Defaults "tests/testthat". strTitle (length-1 character) title issue.

title: qcthat 1.1.2
text: Two new vignettes added, vignette("qcthat") vignette("business_process"). split refined information previously README (#98, #236). slide deck pkgdown site finalized ahead PHUSE US Connect 2026 (#170).

title: qcthat 1.1.2
text: Warnings tests now produce warnings rather errors (#130). Tests longer double-counted associated multiple issues (#180).

title: qcthat 1.1.1
text: patch release contains improvements bug fixes discovered applying experimental AI agent features released qcthat v1.1.0. changes tracked #241.

title: qcthat 1.1.1
text: experimental tag-tests--issues skill now generates report pkgdown/assets describing reasons tests tagged issue. pkgdown uses pkgdown, report available {pkgdown_root}/test_tag_reasons.html. recommend using report locally verify results, deleting report without merging pkgdown site (#242).

title: qcthat 1.1.1
text: FetchRepoIssueClosers() now better determining pull request merge caused issue closed. portions issue-test relationship mechanism properly fetched GitHub (case local repo changes), fetches now much optimized (#243, #251). ExtractTestsFromFiles() now uses much robust mechanism quickly parse tests, built {astgrepr} R package (#247, #257). GetGHRemote() longer truncates repo name repositories . name (“gsm.reporting”) (#248). qcthat.yaml workflow longer erroneously mentions “push” triggers (#259).

title: qcthat 1.1.0
text: New features tools help track user acceptance testing, help use AI agents get started qcthat.

title: qcthat 1.1.0
text: Experimental functions tracking user-acceptance testing (UAT): ExpectUserAccepts() enables tracking user acceptance connecting testthat tests GitHub issues. UAT issue closed, test pass (#65, #111, #113, #115, #120). TriggerUAT() triggers UAT test cycle closed issue. function used improved GitHub Actions framework, installable via Action_qcthat() (#65, #111, #114, #115, #116, #157). Improved onboarding: use_qcthat() calls SetupGHLabels() Action_qcthat(), set repository GitHub Actions workflows labels used QC tracking (#129, #141, #143, #161, #165, #181, #198). Experimental AI agent features: Skill_TagTestsWithIssues() installs AI agent skill tag tests issues, using new functions ExtractTestsFromFiles(), MapTestFilesToPotentialIssues(), PrepareTestIssueContext() (#52, #53, #200, #201, #233).

title: qcthat 1.1.0
text: Additional improvements QC reports, links GitHub Actions run (#150), timestamps (#172), attaching QC Reports releases within release description (#152). Additional changes tracked parent issues (#123, #160). pkgdown site qcthat now includes slide deck introduce package associated system (#166, #167, #168, #169).

title: qcthat 1.1.0
text: Ignored issues filtered reports (#118). Reports work properly active branch associated multiple PRs (#132), PRs contain lot commits (#133), PRs merged (#149). GetGHOwner() GetGHRepo() now work forks, even local repo name match upstream repo name (#199).

title: qcthat 1.0.0
text: can now use package implement QC framework R package associated GitHub repository.

title: qcthat 1.0.0
text: series QC*() functions generate QC reports different sets issues: QCPackage() issues tests R package associated GitHub repository (#46, #69). QCCompletedIssues() issues closed completed (#69, #80). QCIssues() specific issues (#86), QCMilestones() issues associated specific named milestones (#68, #88) QCPR() issues associated specific GitHub pull requests, QCMergeGH() generalize issues associated GitHub merge using GitHub’s graph determine connections, QCMergeLocal() detect probable associations based commit messages (#68, #84). series Action_*() functions set GitHub actions QC reports: Action_QCCompletedIssues() run QCCompletedIssues() (#69, #73). Action_QCPRIssues() run QCPR() specific pull requests (#55, #68). Action_QCMilestone() run QCMilestones() pull requests associated milestones, releases names match milestones (#68, #88).

title: qcthat 1.0.0
text: release also includes number helper functions internal improvements.

title: qcthat 0.2.0
text: complete rewrite package implement framework links GitHub issues evidence issues implemented.

title: qcthat 0.2.0
text: Function FetchRepoIssues() get issues GitHub repository compile user-friendly data frame (#34, #47). Function CompileTestResults() extract test results testthat test runs compile user-friendly data frame (#32). Function CompileIssueTestMatrix() link GitHub issues test results, producing IssueTestMatrix object summarizes state issues associated tests (#35, #49). Custom print methods qcthat_SingleIssueTestResults, qcthat_Milestone, qcthat_IssueTestMatrix objects display contents user-friendly tree format (#31, #36, #45, #60, #61).
